description_long: This chapel is constructed from dark stone blocks, featuring a prominent rectangular bell tower with louvered openings and battlements on one corner. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in dark slate tiles, with multiple gables and intersecting rooflines that add architectural complexity. The front facade displays two large pointed-arch windows flanking a central arched doorway, which is accessed by a small set of steps. The side and rear elevations include additional pointed-arch windows, some of which are larger and more ornate, while others are simpler. The structure is solid and imposing, with thick walls and minimal exterior ornamentation aside from the window shapes and tower details. The overall appearance is reminiscent of late 19th-century Gothic Revival chapels commonly found in urban neighborhoods.
description: A dark stone Gothic Revival chapel with a tall bell tower, steep slate roofs, and pointed-arch windows and doors.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main arched entrance door, two large pointed-arch windows, and the bell tower on the left.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights a large central pointed-arch window and the intersecting gables, with the bell tower visible in the background.
description_view_2: The side view reveals a smaller pointed-arch window and the full length of the chapel, with the bell tower at the far end.
description_view_3: This angled view shows both the main entrance and the bell tower, as well as additional side windows and roof details.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: Present; a square bell tower with louvered openings and battlements is located at the front left corner of the chapel.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is a double-leaf, arched wooden door painted red, set within a pointed stone archway. It is accessed by a small set of steps and lacks ornate decorative elements.
